subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Saturn's Rings
description: A short lesson to help you apply Saturn's Rings.

hook.text: Saturn's rings span 280,000 km but are just 10 meters thick. Stunningly vast yet paper-thin.

buildup.text: They're made of ice and rock, from grains of sand to chunks the size of houses.

discovery.text: Galileo saw them in 1610 but thought Saturn had ears. Huygens identified them as rings in 1655.

twist.text: The rings are only 100 million years old. Dinosaurs walked Earth before Saturn had its rings.

climax.text: Cassini revealed that ring particles constantly collide and clump, making the rings a dynamic system.

punchline.text: Paper-thin and vast. Saturn's rings are younger than dinosaurs.

quiz.question: Why are Saturn's rings considered surprisingly young?
options: They formed only about 100 million years ago, long after the solar system
They were created when humans first appeared on Earth
They are only a few thousand years old according to recent data
